title: -Revenue Agreement: Alameda Alliance for Health Provider Recruitment Initiative

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess to
accept a grant award, and execute any resultant agreements and amendments, in the
amount of $20,000 for Fiscal Year 2026, from the Alameda Alliance for Health Provider
Recruitment Initiative to support the development of the City of Berkeley’s Health,
Housing, and Community Services Department’s Community Health Worker (CHW)
Program.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
Alameda Alliance for Health is mandating that all recipients of the Provider Recruitment
Incentive complete a signed agreement by January 23, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The Health, Housing, and Community Services Department (HHCS) is currently
developing a CHW Program as part of the California Advancing and Innovating Medi-
Cal (CalAIM) initiative. The allocated funds will be used to support implementation
costs related to the CHW certificate training for staff in the Public Health Division.
Training costs of up to two thousand dollars ($2,000) per staff member are eligible for
reimbursement, provided they complete certification that is approved by Alameda
Alliance for Health and comply with Department of Health Care Services (DHCS)
requirements. Funding in the amount of $20,000 will be deposited in One-Time Grants
Fund 336.
Per the terms of the agreement, CHW certificate training must begin within one (1)
year of the Memorandum of Understanding signature date and must be completed
within two (2) years of the MOU signature date, no later than end of Fiscal Year 2028.
Spending of the referenced grant funds is subject to Council approval of the budget
and the Annual Appropriations Ordinances. The grant budget will be adjusted as part
of the Second Amendment to the FY 2026 Annual Appropriations Ordinance.

sections.current_situation:
Community Health Worker Program is a Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing our
goal to be a customer-focused organization that provides excellent, timely, easily-
accessible service and information to the community.
sections.background:
Alameda Alliance is a not-for-profit community-based healthcare organization that
contracts with the State of California to provide Medi-Cal services in Alameda County.
The City of Berkeley is a local health jurisdiction within the Alliance provider network
serving Medi-Cal enrolled adults, children, and families in Berkeley. Over the past
decade, Medi-Cal has undergone significant evolution and expansion, particularly
through the Affordable Care Act (ACA) and various federal and state policy changes.
DHCS has launched several initiatives to enhance the beneficiary experience,
emphasizing the integration of delivery systems, alignment of funding, data reporting,
quality, and infrastructure. This strategic shift led to the creation of California Advancing
and Innovating Medi-Cal, or CalAIM.
DHCS added CHW services as a Medi-Cal benefit starting July 1, 2022. CHW services
are preventive health services to prevent disease, disability, and other health conditions
or their progression; to prolong life; and promote physical and mental health and well-
being. CHW services may assist with a variety of concerns impacting Managed Care
Plan members, including but not limited to, the control and prevention of chronic
conditions or infectious diseases, behavioral health conditions, and need for preventive
services. Additionally, CHW services can help Medi-Cal members receive appropriate
services related to perinatal care, preventive care, sexual and reproductive health,
environmental and climate-sensitive health issues, oral health, aging, injury, and
domestic violence and community violence prevention services.
As a local health jurisdiction with a history of serving high-need Medi-Cal beneficiaries,
HHCS’s participation in CalAIM as CHW providers aligns with its strategic objectives.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental impacts or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
The recommended action provides the City with funding to support staff certification,
which is a prerequisite for providing reimbursable CHW services. Investing in CHW
workforce development strengthens the City’s ability to address health disparities,
improve community engagement, and expand preventive services without drawing on
local funds. Adoption of this recommendation allows the City to fully participate in the
CHW benefit and draw Medi-Cal reimbursement for CHW activities that HHCS already
conducts.

Alliance for Health Provider Recruitment Initiative CONSENT CALENDAR

sections.alternatives:
No alternative actions were identified.
CONTACT PERSON
Tamiko Huey, Senior Management Analyst, HHCS, (510) 725-2243.
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ALAMEDA ALLIANCE FOR HEALTH PROVIDER RECRUITMENT INITATIVE GRANT
AGREEMENT
WHEREAS, the Department of Health Care Services (DHCS), through the California
Advancing and Innovating Medi-Cal (CalAIM) initiative, has created the Community
Health Worker (CHW) benefit to expand preventive and whole-person care services;
and
WHEREAS, the Alameda Alliance for Health Provider Recruitment Incentive funds have
been awarded to the City of Berkeley Health, Housing, and Community Services
Department to support development of the City’s Community Health Worker Program;
and
WHEREAS, this grant agreement requires these funds be used to cover CHW
certificate training costs of up to two thousand dollars ($2,000) per eligible staff member
for a maximum of $20,000 through Alliance-approved programs that meet DHCS
requirements; and
WHEREAS, use of these funds will allow the City to meet state training requirements,
build internal workforce capacity, and prepare to deliver Medi-Cal reimbursable CHW
services at no cost to the General Fund, and
WHEREAS, funding in the amount of $20,000 will be deposited in One-Time Grants
Fund 336 and will be appropriated as part of the Second Amendment to the FY 2026
Annual Appropriations Ordinance.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager or designee to receive the funds
up to the maximum grant award which may be larger or smaller than and the $20,000
and execute any resultant agreements and amendments resulting from this grant
award. A record signature copy of said agreement and any amendments to be on file in
the Office of the City Clerk.
